name: bridge_between_two_titles
patch: If the question asks for a person who is connected to two titles (phrases in quotes or two capitalized title-like spans) and the current query does not include a cast/credit relation, build compact keyword queries by: KEEP both title spans; RESTORE an explicit bridge relation token such as cast OR starring OR appeared OR credited between them; RETAIN any candidate person names already present in the input query; DROP peripheral modifiers (e.g. festival, award, review) that cause topical drift; STYLE the final query as a short keyword string like: "<TitleA> <TitleB> cast starred appeared credited". Apply only when the input lacks an existing "cast/credited" token to avoid changing already-correct queries.
gradient_intent: move queries that only list ambiguous titles toward including an explicit cast/credit bridge so BM25 retrieves person pages or cast lists
preservation_intent: do not change queries that already include cast/credit tokens or explicit person-name candidates

name: person_to_national_side_bridge
patch: When the question asks about a person's national side or asks where that side hosts its home matches (detect phrases like "national team", "played for", "home matches", "home stadium"), produce a compact retrieval query by: KEEP the person's full name token; RESTORE relation tokens: "played for" OR "national team" OR "played for [country]" and stadium/home-host tokens like "home stadium" or "hosts most of its home"; RETAIN country-name candidates if multiple plausible countries are mentioned in the input; DROP club-level or season-level tokens that mislead retrieval; STYLE as a short keyword query: "<PersonName> played for national team home stadium hosts". Only run when the current query does not already contain explicit national-team or home-stadium tokens.
gradient_intent: nudge queries toward combining the person with 'played for' + 'national team' + stadium/home-host tokens so BM25 surfaces the correct national-side page
preservation_intent: avoid altering queries that already target a clear national-team/stadium relation or already retrieve the correct national-side pages

name: ordinal_release_discography_bridge
patch: If the question requests the release date of an ordinal work (detect ordinals like 1st/11th/eleventh) of a named music group or other creative group described with a superlative (e.g. "highest-selling"), and the current query lacks discography/release context, construct a compact query by: KEEP the group's name and the superlative descriptor if present; RESTORE relation tokens: "discography" OR "nth" OR "release date" OR "released on"; RETAIN nearby timeline context (years) if present in the input; DROP unrelated entity mentions that collapse candidate sets; STYLE as a terse keywords string: "<GroupName> discography 11th release released on". Only apply when the query does not already include explicit discography/release-date tokens.
gradient_intent: shift ambiguous group queries toward discography+ordinal+release-date queries so BM25 returns the correct release/record page
preservation_intent: do not change queries that already specify discography/"released" tokens or already target the correct discography page

name: shared_origin_compact_relation
patch: When the question asks what culture/origin two entities share (detect phrasing like "what culture do both", "from the same country", or "which culture" referring to two named items), generate a compact origin/nationality query by: KEEP both entity names; RESTORE relation tokens such as "origin" OR "nationality" OR "culture of" OR "from" placed after the entity names; RETAIN candidate country names if they are ambiguous; DROP descriptive adjectives or plot/location details that cause drift; STYLE as "<EntityA> origin nationality" + "<EntityB> origin nationality" (or a single compact joint query like "<EntityA> <EntityB> origin nationality"). Apply only when there is not already an explicit nationality/origin token present.
gradient_intent: encourage queries to include explicit origin/nationality tokens so BM25 retrieves pages that state country/cultural origin
preservation_intent: avoid touching queries that already retrieve nationality/origin pages or that already include precise country tokens

name: restore_bridge_relation_tokens
patch: Conditional rule: when the question contains a containment/location relation (detect tokens: "which country", "what country", "located in", "part of", "terminus", "ends at", "where" + place-type) but the candidate hop2 query lacks an explicit relation token, append one short relation cue that matches the question (examples: "which country", "located in", "terminus at"). Reversible edit-script template to apply only when a relation is missing: KEEP: primary anchor entity token(s); RESTORE: one compact relation cue (e.g. "which country" / "located in" / "terminus at"); RETAIN: any alternate short place names already present; DROP: higher-level topical tokens only if they conflict with the relation; STYLE: keep query compact and keyword-like. Only trigger if the input query does not already contain a relation cue.
gradient_intent: move queries that lack an explicit bridge relation toward including a compact relation cue (so BM25 retrieves pages that state the containment/terminus relationship)
preservation_intent: do not change queries that already contain appropriate relation cues or that correctly retrieve answers; keep queries compact and keyword-like

name: alias_aka_restore_for_name_lookup
patch: Conditional rule: when the question asks for an alternate name/alias (detect tokens: "another name", "also known as", "aka", "also called", "alternate name") and the hop1 anchor is a named entity, ensure hop2 includes a short alias-cue token ("aka" or "also known as") plus the anchor; if hop1 contained an explicit candidate alias set, preserve those verbatim (do NOT invent aliases). Reversible edit-script template: KEEP: anchor name token(s); RESTORE: alias cue token "aka" or phrase "also known as"; RETAIN: any explicit candidate aliases from hop1; DROP: unrelated topic words that dilute the name search; STYLE: keep query compact (anchor + aka + alias-cue). Only apply when no alias cue already present.
gradient_intent: push queries that are seeking alternate/other names to include an alias cue so BM25 will surface pages listing alternative names or alias sections
preservation_intent: avoid adding aliases when hop1 already provided alias tokens or when no alias-question cue is detected; do not add OR/quote expansions beyond preserving explicit candidate aliases

name: expand_person_property_terms_with_years
patch: Conditional rule: when the question requests a person defined by an event/property (detect tokens like "suspend", "suspended", "suspension", "arrest", "convict", "charged", "ban", "award") and/or includes year(s), and the current hop2 query lists candidate names but no property keywords, append compact property keywords and any year tokens (e.g. "suspended suspension" + "2013 2014"). Reversible edit-script template: KEEP: candidate name tokens or team/role anchors produced by hop1; RESTORE: compact property cue tokens ("suspended"/"suspension"/"arrested" etc.) and question year tokens; RETAIN: the original candidate set (do not invent new names); DROP: seasonal or roster noise that doesn't indicate the property; STYLE: short keyword list (names + property + years). Only apply when hop1 candidates exist and no property keywords are present.
gradient_intent: nudge queries toward including disciplinary/event vocabulary (and year constraints) so BM25 retrieves pages that mention the queried property for those people
preservation_intent: do not broaden the candidate set by adding new names; do not apply when the question is not about a person-event/property or when property tokens already present

name: prune_noisy_higher_level_entities
patch: Conditional rule: when the hop1 query or question includes multiple geographic/hierarchical location tokens (city/county/state/country) and retrieval is drifting to broad topical pages, produce a compact hop2 query that: (a) KEEPs the most specific anchor (city/county/place) from hop1; (b) RESTOREs a single relation cue from the question (e.g. "which country"/"located in"); (c) DROPs overly broad/top-level geopolitical tokens only if the question asks for the lower-level relation. Reversible edit-script template: KEEP: most specific place-anchor token(s); RESTORE: one relation token; RETAIN: alternate short names for the same anchor; DROP: broad top-level location tokens causing drift; STYLE: compact anchor + relation. Only trigger when retrieval shows topic-drift or when multiple level tokens are present and no explicit relation is included.
gradient_intent: reduce topic drift caused by mixing multiple geographic levels by forcing queries to anchor on the specific place and the needed relation token
preservation_intent: avoid removing top-level tokens when the question explicitly asks about them; keep existing good queries unchanged and preserve compact, keyword-like form

name: alias_bridge_restore
patch: If the question requests an "alternate/another name", "also known as", or asks "what is another name for" an organization/ movement/ group: 1) KEEP existing named-entity anchors from the input query (e.g., the group name, country, year). 2) RESTORE compact alias/bridge tokens: append short BM25 cues: aka OR "also known as" OR "also called" and common social-alias markers (hashtag form without punctuation). 3) RETAIN the candidate geographic/time anchors (country, year) if present; do NOT broaden to global ORs. 4) DROP noisy modality words (e.g., verbose phrases like "online campaign" or long narrative clauses) when they cause topic drift. STYLE: produce a compact keyword query that looks like: <GroupName> aka OR "also known as" <country> <year> #hashtag-form. Only apply when the original query lacks alias cues.
gradient_intent: move queries that ask for alternate names toward adding alias-bridge tokens (aka/also known as/hashtag) while keeping the named anchor and context (country/year) to retrieve alias pages.
preservation_intent: do not modify queries that already contain explicit alias signals (aka/"also known as"/quotation of an alias) or that are already precise list/article queries.

name: ordinal_item_list_bridge
patch: When the question asks for an ordinal element of a named recurring/serial work (words like "first/second/third/fourth" or explicit ordinal number plus a series title): 1) KEEP the series/work title token(s) exactly as present. 2) RESTORE compact list-page and ordinal cues: append tokens: list OR "list of" and the series title, numeric season/episode shorthand if implied (e.g., "season 1" or "S1E4"), and plain tokens for the ordinal ("episode 4" in short form). 3) RETAIN any candidate title tokens if the input already includes a plausible candidate set. 4) DROP unrelated topical terms that cause retrieval of other series/lists. STYLE: keep query keyword-like (e.g., <SeriesTitle> list "season 1" S1E4 "episode 4"). Only trigger when the input question is seeking the nth member and the current query lacks explicit list/season/ordinal list-page cues.
gradient_intent: encourage adding short list-page and ordinal tokens (list, season, SxEy, numeric ordinal) so BM25 retrieves list or episode-article pages that contain the ordinal entry.
preservation_intent: avoid changing queries that already include explicit list/season/ordinal tokens or exact-article titles; do not expand to broad OR lists unless preserving an explicit candidate set from the input.

name: location_affiliation_bridge
patch: For questions that ask where an entity/administrative unit is located or which country/state an organization is based in (signals: 'which country', 'based in', 'headquartered', 'part of', 'located in', county/borough tokens): 1) KEEP the place/entity anchor tokens (county name, community name, facility name). 2) RESTORE bridge relation tokens: append short location/affiliation cues such as "located in" OR "part of" OR "based in" OR "headquartered" plus the administrative level token (country OR state OR county). 3) RETAIN the minimal candidate set of plausible organizations if multiple plausible organizations are implied (e.g., keep both named nonprofits in the query only when the question asks to choose between them). 4) DROP noisy topic modifiers that shift retrieval away from the location/affiliation relation (e.g., long history/narrative phrases). STYLE: compact query form like: <PlaceOrEntity> "located in" OR "part of" <country/state> OR <OrganizationName> "based in" <state>. Only add these tokens when the original query lacks an explicit "located/part of/based in" relation.
gradient_intent: push queries to include short location/affiliation bridge cues so BM25 finds pages that state country/state relationships or organizational headquarters.
preservation_intent: do not alter queries already containing explicit location/affiliation tokens or precise targets (e.g., 'headquartered in' or an exact site name); avoid introducing broad country OR expansions when the input already singles out one candidate.

name: last_assignment_station_bridge
patch: When the question requests a "last stationed/last assignment/last station" for a military/unit or similar organizational unit: 1) KEEP the exact unit name anchor as provided. 2) RESTORE concise assignment/station tokens: append short BM25 cues such as "last stationed" OR "stationed at" OR "last assigned" and common facility abbreviations (AFB, AFB variants) or 'Air Force Base' spelled short. 3) RETAIN unit-lineage or sector tokens only if the input already includes them (so you preserve candidate set). 4) DROP long provenance/lineage narrative fragments that bring back other unrelated units. STYLE: compact keyword query like: <UnitName> "last stationed" "stationed at" AFB OR "last assigned". Only apply if the input query lacks explicit "last"/"stationed"/"assigned" tokens.
gradient_intent: nudge queries to add concise 'last stationed/last assigned' bridge phrases and base abbreviations so BM25 returns unit pages that include last-station lines.
preservation_intent: avoid changing queries that already include explicit 'last stationed'/'last assigned' wording or an exact base name; do not add broad OR expansions unless preserving explicit candidate names from the input.

name: restore_term_etymology_bridge
patch: If the question asks for a 'type of' or a name derived from another term (detect tokens: "type", "named after", "term", "name derived", "called"), then when composing the hop2 query: emit a compact edit-script over the current query: KEEP: existing event/entity anchors (proper names, dates, locations). RESTORE: a short etymology/term-origin cue (append keywords: term origin etymology "name derived from" called). RETAIN: ambiguous candidate labels (do not expand them into quoted answers). DROP: peripheral actors or crowd words that cause topic drift (e.g., long lists of associated persons) unless they are the direct anchor. STYLE: keep query keyword-like and short (space-separated keywords). Apply only when the question indicates a term/etymology hop.
gradient_intent: push queries toward adding explicit etymology/term-origin bridge cues so BM25 surfaces pages about word origin/labels (e.g., 'bushwhacker' pages) instead of only general event/actor pages
preservation_intent: do not alter queries for non-etymology questions and avoid adding these cues when the original query already contains an explicit etymology/bridge token

name: explicit_birthplace_bridge
patch: If the question requests a town/city/place of birth (detect: "born", "birthplace", "town", "city", "where was * born"), then edit the hop2 query with this reversible script: KEEP: person name (and birthdate if present) as anchors. RESTORE: a concise birthplace bridge cue (append keywords: born place_of_birth town city suburb early_life). RETAIN: nearby locality candidates only if they appear in first-hop titles; do not invent place names. DROP: non-local biographical facets (albums, film credits) that pull retrieval away. STYLE: keep compact, keyword-like tokens.
gradient_intent: nudge queries to include explicit 'place of birth' tokens so BM25 retrieves locality/early-life biography pages rather than only work/film pages
preservation_intent: avoid changing behavior for queries that already include an explicit 'place of birth' cue or where first-hop passages already give the town

name: nationality_culture_bridge
patch: When the question asks about shared culture, nationality, or origin between entities (detect: "what culture", "culture of", "what nationality", "origin" in relation to two items), append a compact origin/nationality bridge: KEEP: the two entity anchors (city title and film/title). RESTORE: origin/culture cue keywords (append: culture nationality origin "culture of"). RETAIN: film/title or city anchors as candidate selectors. DROP: cast/crew lists or unrelated trivia that dilute the nationality signal. STYLE: keep keyword-like terms, avoid quoted gold labels.
gradient_intent: cause BM25 to prefer pages explicitly stating nationality/origin (e.g., 'Dutch') by surfacing 'culture'/'nationality' pages rather than only plot or crew pages
preservation_intent: do not overwrite queries for questions already seeking works or facts not about nationality; avoid adding nationality keywords when the question is not about culture/origin

name: restore_explicit_relation_verbs
patch: For relation/connection questions (detect phrasing: 'how is X related to Y', 'how is X and Y related', 'what award did the creator of X receive', highway relation queries, or similar), add a minimal relation-verb cue matched to the question type. Use a reversible edit-script: KEEP: original entity anchors. RESTORE: one of these compact relation tokens (choose by question signal): for creators/awards -> "created by" "creator" "recipient" "award"; for infrastructure/routes -> "terminus" "terminates at" "junction" "meets at". RETAIN: narrow candidate set if first-hop titles suggest a small set of persons or routes. DROP: broad lists of unrelated routes or people. STYLE: append only the most relevant relation token (one or two keywords) to keep query compact.
gradient_intent: move queries toward adding explicit relation verbs so BM25 returns pages that state the relation (creator→person and that person's awards; route→terminus/junction statements) instead of ambiguous or unrelated pages
preservation_intent: do not add generic relation tokens when the question already contains the precise relation word, and avoid broad expansions that could change behavior on currently-correct examples

name: restore_parenthetical_disambiguator
patch: When the question names an ambiguous entity variant (contains a year, era, or parenthetical descriptor in the question text) but the current query omits that qualifier, append that disambiguator as a compact parenthetical token. Use this reversible edit-script when applicable: KEEP: main entity tokens; RESTORE: parenthetical qualifier (year / descriptor / region); RETAIN: nearby list/article candidates if present; DROP: side person names not part of the entity title; STYLE: keep result compact and keyword-like. Only apply if the query does not already contain an exact titled page (e.g. a parenthetical or quoted title).
gradient_intent: move queries toward including explicit disambiguators (e.g., year/parenthetical qualifiers) to hit the exact target article instead of generic variants
preservation_intent: do not change behavior when the query already contains a precise titled page or parenthetical disambiguator

name: restore_bridge_relation_token
patch: If the question requests a comparative attribute, debut/first-time, or a specific attribute (words like 'duration', 'first', 'debut', 'episode', 'names', 'population'), ensure the query includes a compact normalized relation token extracted from the question (e.g. duration, 'first contested', 'episode list', names, population). Reversible edit-script: KEEP: subject anchor tokens; RESTORE: normalized relation token(s) derived from the question; RETAIN: candidate-qualifier tokens (park/ride/event) when ambiguous; DROP: unrelated descriptive phrases; STYLE: single-token or short-phrase, keywordy. Only add relation tokens when the query currently lacks them.
gradient_intent: encourage adding the missing bridge/relation keyword so hop2 retrieves passages about the specific attribute (duration, debut, episode lists, population counts)
preservation_intent: avoid changing queries that already contain an explicit relation token or an unambiguous target (preserve current correct BM25 behavior)

name: promote_list_page_candidates_for_name_requests
patch: When the question asks for people/items by name or asks 'which X', 'which MPs', 'names of', or 'list of', append compact list-page cues (e.g. names OR "List of" + subject-type) to preserve the candidate set. Reversible edit-script: KEEP: subject/topic anchor; RESTORE: list-page cue ('List of' OR names token for the subject); RETAIN: ambiguous qualifiers if multiple list targets possible; DROP: single-item entity tokens that collapse the candidate set; STYLE: keep query compact and focused on list/title phrases. Only apply when the input query does not already include a list-page or names cue.
gradient_intent: expand collapsed candidate retrieval toward list/listing pages that contain the requested names rather than single-entity pages
preservation_intent: do not add list cues when the question asks for a single known fact and the query already targets an exact article

name: drop_noisy_side_persons_keep_anchor
patch: If a query contains multiple person-name tokens but the question's main subject is an organization/place/event or a single named anchor, remove side person names that are not the main anchor to reduce topical drift. Reversible edit-script: KEEP: the main anchor/person or organization explicitly asked about; RESTORE: any missing bridge relation tokens if the question implies one; RETAIN: other tokens only if question explicitly asks about those people; DROP: extra person names and noisy same-surname matches; STYLE: keep query compact and entity-focused. Only execute when the extra person tokens are not required by the question.
gradient_intent: reduce retrieval noise from side-person names that pull BM25 away from the intended target article
preservation_intent: avoid removing person tokens if the question explicitly asks about those persons (preserve correctness in name/list queries)

name: restore_compact_relation_cues_for_how_related
patch: If the question asks “how is X and Y related” (or contains the phrase "how is" + two named anchors) then build the hop2 query by: KEEP the two anchor names or short aliases exactly as they appear in the hop1 input; RESTORE a small set of compact relation cue tokens that link pairwise entities (e.g. terminus meets intersection junction ends_at joins intersects) appended as keywords (no long phrases, no quoted OR lists); RETAIN only place tokens that were present in the input anchors (county/city names) if they disambiguate the junction; DROP other nearby same-domain side entities that were retrieved but not named in the question; STYLE: output a short, keyword-like query (anchors + 2–4 relation tokens). Apply this rule only when two anchors are present and the question explicitly asks about their relationship.
gradient_intent: nudge queries toward adding concise bridge relation tokens (terminus/intersection/meet) when the user asks how two entities relate
preservation_intent: do not change queries that already return correct relation answers or that do not ask for an inter-entity relationship

name: restore_usage_and_credit_cues_for_type_of_used_item
patch: If the question pattern is "What is the type of <X> that was used by <Y>" or contains both a subject (user's entity) and an object described as 'used'/'used by', then: KEEP the subject name and any explicit titled item (composition title) from the hop1 input; RESTORE compact usage/credit tokens such as used_in used_by credited arrangement version credited_as recorded_as liner_notes credit (as single keywords); RETAIN multiple candidate item titles only if they are explicitly present in the input; DROP unrelated genre or artist names that were not asked about; STYLE: produce a short keyword query combining the subject, the item title (if present), and 2–3 usage/credit tokens. Only activate when the question requests a 'type' or 'kind' of an item that was 'used' by the subject.
gradient_intent: encourage addition of compact 'usage/credit' bridge cues (arrangement/credited) so hop2 retrieves passages that state the 'type' or 'version' of an item used
preservation_intent: avoid changing queries that already include explicit credit/arrangement tokens or that do not ask about 'type'/'used by' relationships

name: explicit_national_team_to_home_stadium_bridge
patch: When the question asks about 'the national team for which <Person> played' and then asks where that national team 'hosts most of its home matches' (or similar), then: KEEP the national-team / country token discovered in hop1 if present (or keep the person's name if the team name is not yet found); RESTORE compact bridge tokens to turn that team name into a stadium query (e.g. home_stadium home_matches hosts_most_home_matches home_ground plays_most_home_matches); RETAIN candidate stadium names only if they appear in hop1; DROP local club/stadium mentions that are not prefixed by 'national' or 'home'; STYLE: produce a short keyword query like "<team_or_country> home stadium hosts most home matches". Only apply when the question explicitly connects a person -> national team -> home matches.
gradient_intent: move hop2 queries to directly search the team's 'home stadium' phrase when the user asks where a national team hosts home matches
preservation_intent: do not modify hop2 queries for unrelated 'where' questions or for queries already targeting the team's stadium

name: broaden_action_search_when_candidate_set_missing
patch: If the question requests a person-of-role for a named organization who experienced an action in specific years (e.g. 'which <role> for <Org> was suspended in YEARs') and hop1's candidate set contains no entity with that action, then: KEEP the role token and the organization name from the input; RESTORE compact, general supplementary search cues to broaden coverage without adding long OR lists — for example action keywords and timeline cues (suspended suspension suspended_in suspended_years suspension_list 2013 2014) and organizational context tokens (roster transactions list); RETAIN any candidate personal names that appear in the hop1 input (preserve them verbatim in the query) so the model prefers those candidates first; DROP noisy unrelated entities retrieved earlier that do not match the role or action; STYLE: produce a short keyword query combining org + role + action + year tokens (avoid heavy quoting). Only trigger this expansion when a direct match for the requested action is absent from the hop1 summary.
gradient_intent: encourage hop2 to broaden from a narrowly observed candidate set to action+timeline lists (suspension/suspended + years + org) when no candidate in hop1 matches the asked action
preservation_intent: avoid changing queries that already find an entity with the requested action or that successfully return an answer using the original compact candidate set

name: restore_bridge_firsttime
patch: If the question expresses a 'first'/'debut' relationship (contains tokens like: first, debut, first time, first contested, added to), then, for hop2 queries: KEEP primary anchors from the question (event name, host city, venue). RESTORE a small bridge-token set that encodes program-change intent (e.g. added to program | first contested | debut | added YEAR) as plain keywords (not full sentences). DROP unrelated result/medalist/result-history tokens unless the question explicitly asks about winners or results. STYLE: emit a compact, keyword-like conjunctive query (anchors + bridge tokens, use short quoted phrase only when the question gives an exact title). Apply this edit only when 'first/debut/added' appears and the current query lacks explicit bridge tokens.
gradient_intent: nudge queries toward adding explicit program-change / debut bridge terms so BM25 ranks passages about 'added to program' or 'first contested' higher
preservation_intent: do not change queries that already include explicit bridge tokens or already retrieve the correct event; keep queries compact and keyword-like

name: expand_superlative_with_record_qualifiers
patch: If the question uses a superlative with a year (tokens: fastest, highest, longest, earliest + YEAR), then KEEP the superlative and year anchors. RESTORE a concise qualifier set that disambiguates record type (e.g. "top speed" | production car | official record | manufacturer claim | one-way | two-way average) and include 'top speed' when 'fastest' appears. RETAIN multiple plausible record qualifiers together (do not collapse to a single model name). DROP unrelated performance metrics (e.g. 0–60) unless the question mentions acceleration. STYLE: produce a compact BM25-style bag of keywords (no fluent prose). Only apply when the question is ambiguous about record type and the current query lacks these qualifiers.
gradient_intent: push queries to include record-type qualifiers so BM25 retrieves pages that state 'top speed' or 'official record' rather than unrelated performance pages
preservation_intent: avoid changing queries that already contain the correct record-type qualifiers or explicit model names; preserve compact keyword form

name: conjoin_entities_with_relation_token_for_shared_attribute
patch: When the question asks what X and Y 'have in common' (contains phrasings like have in common / both / share / same culture / same nationality), then KEEP both entity names exactly as in the question. RESTORE a small relation-token set (culture | nationality | origin | 'culture of') and conjunctively require it with both anchors. RETAIN short context tags (e.g. 'film' or 'city') only if the question mentions them explicitly; DROP plot/genre words that cause topic drift. STYLE: emit a compact conjunctive keyword query: "X" "Y" culture nationality origin. Apply only for shared-attribute questions and only when the original query lacks an explicit culture/nationality relation token.
gradient_intent: move queries to explicitly target nationality/culture relation so BM25 ranks pages that assert origin/nationality for both entities
preservation_intent: do not alter queries that already conjunctively include the relation token or already retrieve correct shared-attribute passages; keep queries keyword-like and minimal

name: map_person_to_ethnicity_then_census_count
patch: If the question requests 'how many' Americans of the same descent as a named person (phrases: how many Americans are of the descent / of the X descent / 'of the same descent as'), then TRY to detect an ethnicity label in hop1 output (pattern: "PERSON is a(n) ETHNICITY" or "PERSON: ETHNICITY" in retrieved passages). If such a label appears, KEEP the ethnicity token and the year/context if present. RESTORE a census-targeting qualifier set: reporting [ETHNICITY] ancestry | U.S. Census | American Community Survey | population count. RETAIN a specified census year if the question mentions one; DROP unrelated demographic or biographical lists. STYLE: produce a compact keyword query: "reporting [ETHNICITY] ancestry" U.S. Census YEAR. Only apply when the hop1 passages or the question contain a clear ethnicity label; otherwise do not rewrite.
gradient_intent: encourage queries that pivot from a person's label to census/ancestry-count pages (so BM25 finds population-count passages)
preservation_intent: avoid changing queries when no ethnicity label is present in hop1 or when the current query already targets census/ancestry counts; preserve short, keyword-form queries

name: restore_missing_bridge_relation
patch:
Conditional rewrite rule (bridge-recovery): when the question asks a second-hop relation (keywords: where, which country, home, hosts, plays most of its home, how many, who starred, appeared in, etc.) and the hop1 anchor is a named entity from the input, apply a reversible edit-script to the hop2 query: 
- KEEP: preserve the exact anchor tokens (the same casing/spacing as provided) as contiguous keywords. 
- RESTORE: add one short relation cue token sequence that names the missing bridge relation (choose from a small set of compact cues like: "national team", "home stadium", "located in", "population", "cast" or "appeared in"), but add only the single most relevant cue inferred from the question intent. 
- RETAIN: if the input contains an explicit candidate set (alternate names/disambiguators), keep those tokens literally to avoid over-broadening. 
- DROP: drop short ambiguous substrings from the anchor that are common collisions (e.g., short place-name fragments or partial tokens) if they were not present as the exact anchor phrase in the input. 
- STYLE: produce a compact keyword query: [ANCHOR] + [RELATION_CUE] + optional minimal disambiguator (profession/label/year) only when the input provided it. 
Only trigger this rule when hop1 retrieval was noisy or incomplete (many near-miss titles) or when the question explicitly requests the bridge relation; otherwise do not alter the original keyword set.
gradient_intent: push queries to explicitly include a single compact bridge relation token when the second-hop intent is missing (so BM25 matches pages that state the relation: national team, home stadium, located in, population, cast).
preservation_intent: avoid changing queries that already include the correct bridge cue or already return precise anchor disambiguation; keep compact, keyword-like format.

name: prefer_exact_anchor_and_add_disambiguator
patch:
Localized tie-break rule: when a multiword anchor in hop1 has common substring collisions (many retrieved titles share a shorter subword), prefer the exact anchor phrase and add one short disambiguator only if present in the input or question (profession, rank, 'county', 'footballer', 'band', 'school', 'senator', etc.). Implement as reversible edit-script: 
- KEEP: the exact multiword anchor token sequence from the input. 
- RESTORE: if the input or question implies a role/label, append that single-word disambiguator (profession/label) to the query. 
- RETAIN: keep any explicit alternate spellings or diacritics from the input. 
- DROP: remove common shorter substrings that caused noisy matches (do not add new broad synonyms). 
- STYLE: keep the final query compact: "[EXACT_ANCHOR] [ROLE] [BRIDGE_CUE_if_needed]". 
Only apply when retrieval shows many unrelated short-substring matches.
gradient_intent: move BM25 weight toward exact-match anchor pages by adding minimal disambiguation instead of broad expansions that pull noisy topics.
preservation_intent: do not change queries that already use exact-anchor matches or that are already concise and correct.

name: narrow_population_and_statistical_queries
patch:
Statistical-intent guard: when the question begins with or implies a numeric/statistical ask (phrases: how many, number of, population, how many Americans, count of X descent), produce a tight census/statistics-oriented relation token instead of broad group labels. Reversible edit-script: 
- KEEP: the ethnic/ancestry label or demographic noun from hop1 (e.g., 'Danish', 'Danish American', 'X descent'). 
- RESTORE: append one compact statistic-retrieval cue such as "population", "U.S. Census", "American Community Survey", or "reporting [ANCESTRY] ancestry" — choose the single best match for the country in question. 
- RETAIN: if the question names a year or survey, keep that year token. 
- DROP: avoid adding umbrella-group tokens (e.g., broader regional groupings) unless the input explicitly included them. 
- STYLE: output a short keyword query: "[ANCESTRY_LABEL] population U.S. Census [year?]". 
Trigger only on numeric/statistical question forms; otherwise leave hop2 queries unchanged.
gradient_intent: steer BM25 toward census/statistics pages and away from generic overview pages that report aggregated or unrelated ancestry totals.
preservation_intent: do not alter non‑statistical retrievals or queries that already target a specific census/survey source.

name: intersection_of-works_appearance_rule
patch:
Generic 'shared-appearance' pattern: when the question asks for a person who appears in both of two works (signals: "both", "starred in both", "appeared in both", "also starred in"), construct compact intersection queries by requesting cast/credits for each work and preserving disambiguation tokens. Reversible edit-script: 
- KEEP: both work titles exactly as given in the input. 
- RESTORE: append the single compact cue "cast" or "credited" to each work's subquery (do not expand into long OR lists). 
- RETAIN: if the input supplies disambiguators (year, "miniseries", "TV series"), keep those tokens to keep the candidate set narrow. 
- DROP: remove unrelated topical fragments from either title that caused retrieval drift. 
- STYLE: emit two short keyword subqueries joined by an implicit intersection intent (format: "[WORK_A] cast" ; "[WORK_B] cast" → intersection). Only apply when both works are present in the input question.
gradient_intent: encourage retrieval of cast/credit pages for both works so downstream intersection logic can find common names, rather than retrieving general pages about unrelated topics.
preservation_intent: do not modify queries that already include explicit cast/credit cues or that are already narrowly disambiguated; keep queries compact and avoid domain-specific heavy heuristics.

name: restore_bridge_affiliation
patch: If the question asks for a shared institution/organization/affiliation or 'in common' between two named entities, produce a compact keyword query that: KEEP the original named anchors (both entities); RESTORE a short bridge-relation cue such as affiliated OR affiliation OR member OR representing OR based at placed between them; RETAIN any ambiguous disambiguator tokens already present in the input (parenthetical qualifiers); DROP long natural-language phrasing or unrelated modifiers; STYLE: keep the whole query as a compact keyword sequence (no full sentences). Only apply when both entity anchors are present and question wording signals a shared affiliation.
gradient_intent: push queries to add an explicit affiliation/bridge cue between two anchors so BM25 retrieves organization pages (restore missing bridge relation)
preservation_intent: do not change behavior for queries already returning correct organization pages; keep original anchors and parenthetical qualifiers intact

name: preserve_candidate_list_with_source
patch: When the question names a discriminating source (publication/film/album/issue-like noun) or asks 'who'/'what name' among several candidate persons/items, produce a compact query that: KEEP the source name as an anchor (e.g., the magazine/issue token) and the candidate names verbatim; RESTORE a short discriminator token such as featured OR 'featured in' OR cover OR interview OR profile next to the source; RETAIN the exact candidate list as a compact OR clause only if that list came from the input 'candidate' context; DROP added synonym-expansions or free‑form question text; STYLE: keep bracketless, keyword-like formulation and avoid broad OR expansions unless preserving the input candidate set.
gradient_intent: encourage queries that anchor the discriminating source and the explicit candidate set so BM25 surfaces passages that link the source to one candidate (fix over-collapsed/missing source cue)
preservation_intent: avoid changing queries that already preserve an explicit candidate list and the source anchor

name: drop_noisy_side_entities
patch: If a query contains many high-frequency auxiliary or comparative terms (e.g., duration/compare/versus or multiple unrelated place names) that dilute retrieval focus, apply a conservative edit: KEEP the primary object/ride/item or named anchor from the question; DROP peripheral comparison targets and noisy venue/location tokens unless the question explicitly asks for the comparison metric; RESTORE a concise location cue only when the question asks 'located in' or 'hosted at' (use tokens located OR location OR hosted); RETAIN any small necessary comparator (one other anchor) if the question's semantics require it; STYLE: keep the query short and keyword-like to favor BM25 exact-title matches.
gradient_intent: move queries away from noisy, multi-topic formulations toward focused anchor+location/host queries so BM25 retrieves the correct location pages
preservation_intent: do not alter queries that are already focused landmark-location queries or that explicitly require multiple-comparison anchors

name: expand_initialism_creator_bridge
patch: When the question contains an uppercase acronym/initialism (3–6 letters) or a short label (e.g., 'HTO') and asks about origin/creator/award/creator-of, produce a compact query that: KEEP the exact acronym token; RESTORE bridge cues such as 'created by' OR 'creator' OR 'founded by' OR 'established by' OR 'recipient of' adjacent to the acronym; RETAIN probable creator/recipient tokens (person names) only if they appear explicitly in the input candidate set; DROP speculative natural-language expansions; STYLE: keep it keyword-like (acronym + bridge cue + creator/award terms) to retrieve definition/creator pages rather than unrelated topical pages. Apply only when acronym form is present and the question requests a creator/award/origin.
gradient_intent: encourage retrieval of pages that define the acronym and list its creator/awards by adding concise 'created by'/'recipient' bridge tokens
preservation_intent: avoid changing queries that already find definition/creator pages or that do not contain an acronym/initialism

name: work_title_bridge_tag
patch: If the input query contains a multi-word or quoted title (heuristic: >1 title-case tokens or quotes) and the question is asking for a person/entity associated with that title (question words include: who, which person, who sang/performed/starring/co‑star), then append a compact bridge cue rather than extra collaborators. Concretely: KEEP the original title tokens; RESTORE by appending one of the short bridge tokens: performed by | credited to | creator | starring (choose the single best match that fits the question); RETAIN any explicit candidate list supplied by the user (OR or quoted names); DROP unrelated collaborator names only when the bridge cue is appended. Always produce a terse, keyword-only final query (no sentences). Only apply when the query lacks an explicit performer/creator cue already. This edit is reversible by re-inserting dropped collaborator tokens if the original query explicitly listed them as candidates.
gradient_intent: shift queries about creative titles toward adding an explicit performer/creator bridge token so BM25 matches the work->person page instead of drifting to collaborators or label pages
preservation_intent: do not change queries that already include a performer/creator/’born’ bridge cue or that explicitly enumerate candidate names with OR/quotes

name: person_birth_bridge_cue
patch: When the question requests a birthdate/year (contains keywords like born, birth year, date of birth, when was) but the query only references the person indirectly (via a production title, co-star, or role) and the query lacks DOB tokens, append minimal DOB bridge tokens. Concretely: KEEP production title and co-star tokens; RESTORE by adding born | birth year | date of birth | DOB and, if a production title is present, add a compact tie token cast | co‑star | cast list; RETAIN any explicit actor candidate set present; DROP unrelated role descriptors. Keep the query compact (keywords only). Apply only if no DOB bridge token is already present.
gradient_intent: nudge indirect actor/co‑star queries to include DOB search cues so BM25 retrieves actor biography pages (for birth year extraction) instead of only production pages
preservation_intent: avoid changing queries that already contain born/DOB tokens or that explicitly target a single known person

name: organization_lifecycle_cues
patch: If the question asks about the duration, lifespan or existence span of an organization (keywords: how long, lasted, duration, lifespan, dissolved, formed, when formed) and the query contains an organization name and a year but no lifecycle tokens, append compact lifecycle tokens. Concretely: KEEP the org name and leader tokens; RESTORE by appending formation date | formed | dissolution date | dissolved | founded (choose the minimal matching token); RETAIN ambiguous year ranges if present; DROP peripheral place/entity tokens that do not help lifecycle matching. Final query must remain keyword-like. Only apply when no lifecycle token is already present.
gradient_intent: move organization queries toward lifecycle/date pages so BM25 returns formation/dissolution facts (instead of lists or other unrelated org pages)
preservation_intent: do not alter queries that already include explicit lifecycle tokens or that already list exact year candidates

name: prune_noisy_collaborators_preserve_candidates
patch: When a title-based query includes multiple named collaborators/associated entities and retrieval shows topic drift toward those collaborators, prefer a compact performer/creator bridge and prune noisy collaborators unless they were explicitly supplied as candidates. Concretely: KEEP title tokens; if >1 collaborator tokens are present and no performer/creator cue exists then RESTORE by appending performed by | credited to | creator (one token) and DROP extra collaborator tokens; RETAIN collaborators if the original query used explicit candidate notation (OR, quotes) — in that case preserve them. STYLE: result must be short, keyword-like. Record removed tokens so the edit is reversible if candidate-preservation triggers later.
gradient_intent: reduce false positives from collaborator/label noise by replacing multiple side-entities with a single bridge cue so BM25 targets the canonical work->creator page
preservation_intent: do not prune or alter queries that already explicitly mark collaborators as candidates, or queries that already contain a performer/creator bridge token
